instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
What Are The Components Of Life Insurance?
Buying life insurance is often presented as one of the important pillars of an individual’s financial stability as well as their family’s wellbeing, and rightfully so. Without a life cover, one might risk leaving a gaping hole in the financial security of one’s loved ones. This could mean that their family members, especially the ones dependent on them, might be left without support if any unfortunate circumstance were to occur. Thus, it is important to consider buying life insurance. Let’s first understand the components of life insurance such as a death benefit, premiums, riders, and more.
What is Life Insurance?
To start with, there are various types of life insurance plans, each one slightly different from another. However, all of these are designed to offer a life cover to the life assured. So, when one buys a life insurance policy for themselves, they are fortifying the chances of their loved ones being financially stable if they were to face any dire circumstances in the future, which are covered by the policy.
One should first decide which type of life insurance plan is suitable for them. Once they shortlist the plans they like, they can use a life insurance premium calculator corresponding to the plan. This should help them get a better idea of the premiums they may be required to pay for the sum assured and the plan duration they desire.
They can then buy the plan online or offline, as many plans these days are available through both modes. Once the plan purchase is made, you will be required to start paying premiums, as per the chosen payment frequency. The life insurance premium payment frequency can be chosen from the available options. It is advisable to choose a frequency that is suitable for you and does not put any financial pressure on you.
For as long as the policy is in force, you will have a life cover protecting your loved ones financially. Know that riders are also available with the plans. Life insurance riders are add-on features that can allow you to gain more from your policy. Available at an additional cost, they can expand the scope of the policy and allow you to have a policy that covers more possibilities.
Apart from the death benefit, some policies also offer a maturity benefit. This can be claimed if the life assured survives the policy duration. Plans like ULIPs offer a unique combination of life insurance coverage as well as wealth creation via investments in different funds. These investments are prone to market risk, and thus, one should assess one’s risk appetite before buying such a plan.
Components of Life Insurance
After having a brief understanding of how life insurance policies work, one should take a look at the various components of life insurance. These are elements that make up your life insurance plan. It is important to take note of these as they will help you understand how your plan works, which elements play what role, and what you can expect from your policy.
Let’s take a look at the primary components of life insurance policies.
Death Benefit
This is one of the core components of life insurance. In other words, it is the primary benefit offered by the policy, irrespective of the type of policy it is. It is the benefit that the nominee of the policy can claim in the event of death of the life assured. If such an event occurs during the term of the policy, the nominee can initiate a claim.
Sum Assured
Another significant components of life insurance, ‘sum assured’, refers to the amount of coverage that the policy offers. This determines the amount that the nominee may be able to receive if they were to make a claim. Usually, the policy buyer can choose the sum assured. It is often recommended that the sum assured amount should be at least 10x the current annual income of the policyholder1. Choosing such an amount may help fortify the financial security of the nominee or beneficiary in the event of the untimely passing of the person insured.
Premiums
This is what you are required to pay the insurance provider to buy and maintain your policy throughout its duration. The premium payment frequency can be monthly, quarterly, biannually, or yearly. Some policy providers may also offer the option to pay a single lump sum premium for the policy at the time of purchase. Before you buy the plan, ensure that you use an online life insurance calculator to get an idea of the premiums you would be expected to pay for the plan specifications you desire.
Maturity benefit
This component of life insurance is not present with all types of life insurance policies. For example, you may not find a maturity benefit with level term plans, but you may get the same with endowment plans. Maturity benefit is the benefit the policyholder could claim if the life assured survives the policy duration. This benefit may help you support your future financial goals alongside those of your loved ones. If you are keen on a maturity benefit, ensure that you choose a policy that offers it.
Riders
Life insurance riders are add-on features that can help you enhance the scope of your policy. When you add a rider to your policy, it covers more possibilities that you may encounter during the policy period. For example, when you choose a critical illness rider, you can get financial support if the life assured were to be diagnosed with a critical illness covered by the rider. This may help keep the financial burden off the dependents and may also help the life assured avoid making a dent in their savings.
Here are some of the commonly available life insurance riders:
Accidental Death
Accidental Permanent/Partial Disability
Critical Illness
Waiver of Premium
Income Benefit
When choosing a rider, remember that they come at an additional cost. Ensure that you choose life insurance riders that are right for you, and which will help you fortify your policy.
Nomination
You may have heard the term ‘nominee’ mentioned often when it comes to life insurance or several other financial products. What is a nominee? To put it simply, the nominee is the person who will be able to claim the death benefit of the life insurance policy. The policyholder can choose to name multiple nominees. The process of assigning a nominee is known as ‘nomination’. Nomination is usually done at the time of policy purchase. However, you can also change your nominee anytime during the term of the policy.
These are some of the major components of life insurance that you should be aware of. When buying a policy, think of these elements. They can help you imagine the bigger picture of how your policy may benefit you as well as your nominee.
References:
1https://www.livemint.com/money/personal-finance/opt-for-a-sum-assured-of-at-least-10-times-your-annual-income-11619726627295.html

output.final_text:
What Are The Components Of Life Insurance?
Buying life insurance is one of the important aspect of an individual’s financial stability as well as their family’s wellbeing, and rightfully so. Without a life cover, one might risk leaving a gaping hole in the financial security of one’s loved ones in case of their untimely demise. This could mean that their family members, especially the ones dependent on them, might be left without support if any unfortunate circumstance were to occur. Thus, it is important to consider buying life insurance. Let’s first understand the components of life insurance such as a death benefit, premiums, riders, and more.
What is Life Insurance?
To start with, there are various types of life insurance plans, each one slightly different from another. However, all of these are designed to offer a life cover to the life assured. So, when one buys a life insurance policy for themselves, they are strengthening the chances of their loved ones being financially stable if they were to face any dire circumstances in the future, which are covered by the policy.
One should first decide which type of life insurance plan is suitable for them. Once they shortlist the plans they like, they can use a life insurance premium calculator. This should help them get a better idea of the premiums they may be required to pay for the sum assured and the plan duration they desire.
They can then buy the plan online or offline, as many plans these days are available through both modes. Once the plan purchase is made, you will be required to start paying premiums, as per the chosen payment frequency. The life insurance premium payment frequency can be chosen from the available options. It is preferable to choose a frequency that is suitable for you and does not put any financial pressure on you.
As long as the policy is in force, you will have a life cover protecting your loved ones financially in case of your unfortunate demise. Know that riders are also available with the plans. Life insurance riders are add- on features that can allow you additional benefits and is linked with the base policy. Available at an additional cost, they can expand the scope of the policy and allow you to have a policy that covers more possibilities.
Apart from the death benefit, some policies also offer a maturity benefit. This can be claimed if the life assured survives the policy duration. Plans like ULIPs offer a unique combination of life insurance coverage as well as wealth creation via investments in different market linked funds. These investments are prone to market risk, and thus, one should assess one’s risk appetite before buying such a plan.
Components of Life Insurance
After having a brief understanding of how life insurance policies work, one should take a look at the various components of life insurance. These are elements that make up your life insurance plan. It is important to take note of these as they will help you understand how your plan works, which elements play what role, and what you can expect from your policy.
Let’s take a look at the primary components of life insurance policies.
 Death Benefit This is one of the core components of life insurance. In other words, it is the primary benefit offered by the policy, irrespective of the type of policy it is. It is the benefit that the nominee of the policy can claim
in the event of death of the life assured. If such an event occurs during the term of the policy, the nominee can initiate a claim.
 Sum Assured Another significant components of life insurance, ‘sum assured’, refers to the amount of coverage that the policy offers. This determines the amount that the nominee would receive if they were to make a claim. Usually, the policy buyer can choose the sum assured. Choosing such an amount may help strengthen the financial security of the nominee in the event of the untimely passing of the person insured during policy term, provided all due premiums are paid.
 Premiums This is what you are required to pay the insurance provider to buy and maintain your policy throughout its duration. The premium payment frequency can be monthly, quarterly, biannually, or yearly. Some policy providers may also offer the option to pay a single lump sum premium for the policy at the time of purchase. Before you buy the plan, you may use an online life insurance calculator to get an idea of the premiums you would be expected to pay for the plan specifications you desire.
 Maturity benefit This component of life insurance is not present with all types of life insurance policies. For example, you may not find a maturity benefit with pure term plans, but you may get the same with endowment plans. Maturity benefit is the benefit the policyholder could claim if the life assured survives the policy duration. This benefit may help you support your future financial goals alongside those of your loved ones. If you are keen on a maturity benefit, ensure that you choose a policy that offers it.
 Riders Life insurance riders are add-on features that can help you enhance the scope of your policy. When you add a rider to your policy, it covers more possibilities that you may encounter during the policy period. For example, when you choose a critical illness rider, you can get financial support if the life assured were to be diagnosed with a critical illness covered by the rider. This may help keep the financial burden off the dependents and may also help the life assured avoid making a dent in their savings.
Here are some of the commonly available life insurance riders, among others:
o Accidental Death o Accidental Permanent/Partial Disability o Critical Illness o Waiver of Premium o Income Benefit
When choosing a rider, remember that they come at an additional nominal cost. Ensure that you choose life insurance riders that are suitable for your needs.
 Nomination You may have heard the term ‘nominee’ mentioned often when it comes to life insurance or several other financial products. What is a nominee? To put it simply, the nominee is the person who will be able to claim the death benefit of the life insurance policy. The policyholder can choose to name multiple nominees. The process of appointing a nominee is known as ‘nomination’. Nomination is usually done at
the time of policy purchase. However, you can also change your nominee anytime during the term of the policy.
These are some of the major components of life insurance that you should be aware of. When buying a policy, think of these elements. They can help you imagine the bigger picture of how your policy may benefit you as well as your nominee.
